title: urtica domingensis var. domingensis
intro: Urtica domingensis var. domingensis, also known as West Indian nettle or Jamaican nettle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, characterized by its stinging hairs and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions, including arthritis, gout, and wounds. The leaves and roots of Urtica domingensis var. domingensis contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and provide numerous health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye.
